questions.q: The current through the $3 \Omega$ resistor (as shown in figure) is 2 A . Then the value of $V_A-V_B$ will be
questions.solution: Direction of current from right of left

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The galvanometer shown in the figure has resistance $10 \Omega$. It is shunted by a series combination of a resistance $S=1 \Omega$ and an ideal cell of emf 2 V. A current 2A passes as shown. Then choose the correct statement(s).
options: The reading of the galvanometer is 1 A
The reading of the galvanometer is zero
The potential difference across the resistance S is 1.5 V
The potential difference across the resistance S is 2 V
questions.solution: In loop ABCDA $10 i+2-(2-i)=0$ $i=0$ Potential difference across $\mathrm{S} \rightarrow(2-\mathrm{i}) 1=2 \mathrm{~V}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: In the given circuit all ammeters are ideal. Then choose correct statement.
options: Reading of $\mathrm{A}_2$ and Reading of $\mathrm{A}_4$ both are zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Figure shows a copper wire of nonuniform cross section carrying current toward right for sectors $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}$ and C .
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{i} \rightarrow \text { will same } \\
& \mathrm{i}=\text { neA } \mathrm{Vd} \\
& \mathrm{~J}=\frac{\mathrm{i}}{A}, \mathrm{j}^2 \sigma E
\end{aligned}$
Current through the wire is same $i_A=i_B=i_C \because \mathrm{i}=\mathrm{neAV}_{\mathrm{d}}$ and $J=\frac{i}{A}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: The wire $A B$ of a meter bridge changes linearly from radius $r$ to $2 r$ from left end to right end. Length of wire is 1 m . Where should the free end of the galvanometer be connected on $A B$ so that the deflection in the galvanometer is zero?
